Title: PHYTOCHROME-INTERACTING FACTOR 4/HEMERA-mediated thermosensory growth requires the Mediator subunit MED14

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seedling hypocotyl and other thermoresponsive aerial tissues
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes thermomorphogenetic growth including hypocotyl elongation, petiole elongation, and leaf hyponasty
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for warm temperature-induced expression of auxin-related genes and thereby promotes thermosensory growth
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. med14 mutants showed strongly reduced thermomorphogenetic hypocotyl elongation, petiole elongation, and leaf hyponasty at warm temperature. 2. MED14 was required for thermal induction of key auxin-related PIF4 targets including YUC8, IAA19, and IAA29. 3. MED14 physically interacted with PIF4 and HMR and its promoter association at IAA19 and IAA29 required functional PIF4 and HMR.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant phenotyping; transgenic complementation with 35S::MED14-HA; RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; GST pull-down; BiFC; ChIP-qPCR
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript required and function evident in 4-day-old seedlings under continuous red light and warm temperature treatment
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Moderately elevated ambient temperature (27°C versus 20°C); continuous red light; continuous white light; low R/FR shade condition
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S CaMV constitutive promoter used for complementation/overexpression
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential node to manipulate temperature-responsive growth programs, but likely unsuitable as a simple engineering target because MED14 is a central general transcriptional coactivator with broad developmental roles
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad pleiotropic effects expected due to essential role in Mediator-dependent transcription; reduced growth and developmental defects possible when perturbed
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MED14 had only marginal effect on HMR protein abundance but contributed partially to PIF4 transcript/protein accumulation; classified here as Common Gene because it is a transcriptional coactivator/Mediator subunit rather than a pathway enzyme or sequence-specific regulator.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seedling hypocotyl and other thermoresponsive aerial tissues
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Auxin biosynthesis and auxin signaling gene activation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: YUC8; IAA19; IAA29; ST2A; APD7; ARGOS; SAUR genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes warm temperature-induced hypocotyl elongation by activating auxin-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PIF4 is required for thermomorphogenetic growth and for induction of many warm-responsive genes. 2. PIF4 directly targets and activates key auxin-related genes such as YUC8, IAA19, and IAA29 under warm temperature. 3. PIF4 physically interacts with MED14 and is required for MED14 association with IAA19 and IAA29 promoters.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping (pif4-2); RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; GST pull-down; BiFC; ChIP-qPCR; immunoblot
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with HMR and recruits/associates with Mediator through MED14
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Moderately elevated ambient temperature; light environment
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 27°C warm temperature; continuous red light; continuous white light; shade/low R/FR discussed in context
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Warm temperature-induced in seedlings; peak protein accumulation after thermal transition
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central activator/master regulator of thermoresponsive auxin-related growth gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for tuning temperature-responsive architecture and growth, but deployment requires caution because of broad pleiotropic growth effects
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered thermomorphogenesis and shade responses; broad developmental pleiotropy likely
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PIF4 protein accumulation was reduced but not eliminated in med14; PIF4 carries a transcription activation domain important for transactivation.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Transcriptional co-activator; nuclear/plastid dual-localized protein with 9aaTAD
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seedling hypocotyl and other thermoresponsive aerial tissues
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Auxin biosynthesis and auxin signaling gene activation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: YUC8; IAA19; IAA29; other PIF4/HMR/MED14-dependent thermo-induced genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes activation of PIF4 target genes at warm temperature
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for thermo-induced expression of auxin-related growth genes and for proper thermomorphogenetic growth
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HMR is required, together with PIF4, for warm temperature-induced expression of auxin-related genes. 2. HMR physically interacts with MED14 in GST pull-down assays. 3. HMR is required for efficient MED14 promoter occupancy and for MED14 transcript abundance under some temperature regimes, thereby supporting thermo-induced transcriptional activation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping (hmr-22); RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; GST pull-down; ChIP-qPCR; immunoblot
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with PIF4 and MED14/Mediator in a transcriptional activation module
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Moderately elevated ambient temperature; light environment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Required in seedlings during warm temperature responses; HMR transcript less induced context-dependent
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: HMR supports MED14 transcript abundance under lower temperature/short warm treatment conditions
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential coactivator enabling PIF4-mediated activation of auxin-related thermo-induced genes
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune temperature-responsive growth, but likely high pleiotropic risk because of dual localization and broad transcriptional roles
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong pleiotropic defects expected due to effects on PIF4 accumulation, MED14 abundance, and broader thermomorphogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: BiFC with MED14 was not detected, likely due to technical/localization limitations; hmr-22 reduced MED14-HA transcript and protein abundance.
